applicability_notes: Creates a tensor with the same shape/dtype/layout as input, filled with fill_value. Supports writing into a preallocated optional_tensor (in-place output) without allocating new device buffers. Golden: torch.full_like(input_tensor, fill_value).

placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.from_torch(torch_input_tensor, dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device); default interleaved DRAM memory config
unit test: output asserted on-device (ttnn.is_tensor_storage_on_device); when optional_tensor is supplied, no new buffer pages are allocated (output reuses preallocated buffer)
model call site: ttnn.full_like(position_idxs, fill_value=-1) on mesh_device with dtype=ttnn.int32, mesh_mapper for replication; used to build cached 'neg_one' state tensor
